reasoning: Both audits confirm PKGBUILD is standard and safe: sources use HTTPS with pinned checksums, package() only installs binaries/docs. Man pages are non-executable and irrelevant to security.
coverage_issues: qwen missed reviewing man pages (low risk but inconsistent with deepseek)
Neither reviewed .install hooks (none exist in package)
false_positives: deepseek's man page reviews: Overly cautious as troff files can't execute during makepkg
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Skip non-executable docs (man pages, READMEs) unless referenced in PKGBUILD/.install
Enforce mandatory review of .install hooks when present
Optimize resource usage: Avoid wasting tokens on low-risk files
Clarify prompt: Explicitly state that static documentation requires minimal scrutiny
